_comment: Data-driven trigger map for hooks/suggest-skill.sh. Each rule fires a system-reminder nudging the model toward a plugin skill instead of inline reimplementation. Edit this file to add / tune rules — the shell script is intentionally dumb about the mappings.

bash_command.why: drafts the PR body (Why / What / Tested / Demo) with a Conventional Commits title and auto-linked issues
creates the worktree with the project's issue-first branch naming and prefetches issue context via gh
captures the Play-mode screenshot AND embeds it in the PR Demo section — you still get to pick the moment / scene
records with Unity Recorder + uploads via gh attach, with automatic GIF fallback and end-to-end verification the PR renders a real <video> element
closes the current Editor, opens on the target path, waits for MCP correctly (avoids the 30-min curl-hang footgun), and rebinds the session
batchmode Standalone build with the project's BuildScript, per-target output paths, and log-error surfacing
runs the specified PlayMode test class via the warm Editor when available (~2s startup) instead of batchmode's cold ~30s
already covers every diagnostic path for Coplay's Unity MCP (uv, pyenv shims, port 8080, stale stdio registration)
the switch skill kills the specific Editor process (not Unity Hub / license client / MPPM clones), then reopens on the target

user_prompt.why: drafts the PR body — trigger this before hand-writing a description
the Play-mode screenshot + PR-body embed is one skill call away
Unity Recorder + gh attach path, with the correct GIF fallback and rendered-<video>-tag verification
creates the isolated worktree off the default branch with the project's naming convention
close-current + open-new + wait-for-MCP + rebind, in one call
the batchmode Standalone build path with per-target BuildScript methods
